title: lodhra caudata
intro: Lodhra caudata, also known as Lodh or Symplocos racemosa, is a species of flowering plant in the family Symplocaceae. It is widely used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Lodhra caudata include treating menstrual disorders, menopausal symptoms, and skin conditions such as acne and eczema, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wounds and ulcers. Herbal preparations of Lodhra caudata include decoctions, powders, and ointments, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions.
